INS 102.name: Tartrazine
INS 102.notes: Linked to hyperactivity in children. Banned in some countries.

INS 110.name: Sunset Yellow FCF
INS 110.notes: Linked to hyperactivity in children. Banned in Norway and Finland.

INS 124.name: Ponceau 4R
INS 124.notes: Banned in USA and Norway. Linked to hyperactivity.

INS 133.name: Brilliant Blue FCF
INS 133.notes: Artificial colour. Banned in several European countries.

INS 211.name: Sodium Benzoate
INS 211.notes: Forms benzene (carcinogen) when combined with Vitamin C. Linked to hyperactivity.

INS 220.name: Sulphur Dioxide
INS 220.notes: Can trigger asthma. Must be declared if >10mg/kg.

INS 250.name: Sodium Nitrite
INS 250.notes: Can form nitrosamines (carcinogens) in the body. Common in processed meats.

INS 407.name: Carrageenan
INS 407.notes: May cause inflammation and digestive issues in some people.

INS 412.name: Guar Gum
INS 412.notes: Generally safe. May cause bloating in large amounts.

INS 471.name: Mono and Diglycerides of Fatty Acids
INS 471.notes: May contain trans fats not listed on label. Common in baked goods.

INS 508.name: Potassium Chloride
INS 508.notes: Generally safe. Used as sodium substitute.

INS 621.name: Monosodium Glutamate
INS 621.notes: May cause headaches and sensitivity in some people (MSG sensitivity).

INS 627.name: Disodium Guanylate
INS 627.notes: Often used with MSG to amplify effect. Not suitable for gout patients.

INS 631.name: Disodium Inosinate
INS 631.notes: Often used with MSG. Not suitable for gout patients. May be derived from meat/fish.

INS 635.name: Disodium 5-Ribonucleotides
INS 635.notes: Combination of INS 627 and INS 631. MSG-family enhancer. Not suitable for gout patients or people avoiding MSG.

INS 950.name: Acesulfame Potassium
INS 950.notes: Artificial sweetener. Some studies suggest potential metabolic effects.

INS 951.name: Aspartame
INS 951.notes: Classified as 'possibly carcinogenic' by WHO IARC 2023. Not suitable for phenylketonuria patients.

INS 952.name: Cyclamate
INS 952.notes: Banned in USA. Linked to bladder cancer in animal studies.

INS 954.name: Saccharin
INS 954.notes: Oldest artificial sweetener. Some studies suggest gut microbiome disruption.

INS 955.name: Sucralose
INS 955.notes: Generally considered safe but some studies suggest gut microbiome effects.

INS 322I.name: Lecithin
INS 322I.notes: Usually derived from soy or sunflower. Generally safe. May be GMO soy-derived.

INS 503II.name: Ammonium Bicarbonate
INS 503II.notes: Common raising agent in biscuits. Generally safe.

INS 472E.name: Diacetyl Tartaric Acid Esters of Mono and Diglycerides
INS 472E.notes: Synthetic emulsifier. May be derived from animal fats.

INS 223.name: Sodium Metabisulphite
INS 223.notes: Sulphite preservative. Known allergen — triggers asthma. Must be declared if present above 10mg/kg.
